strengths: Demonstrated distributed-systems depth at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, operating microservices at millions of HTTP requests per second with real-time, low-latency constraints
Early-stage agentic-AI exposure at re:cinq, including MCP framework development and tooling for agentic workflows, showing relevant directional momentum
Broad polyglot background across fintech-adjacent companies (RIDE Capital) and event-driven open-source platform work, indicating system-design range
concerns: No demonstrated engineering leadership experience — career history shows no team management, org scaling, or executive ownership; the role requires scaling teams from 10 to 30+ and building a senior leadership layer

Agentic AI involvement is recent (April 2025), limited to tooling and developer infrastructure rather than production multi-agent systems reasoning over live financial data with regulatory consequences

No evidence of fintech-infrastructure depth in credit systems, real-time credit risk, working-capital allocation, or compliance architecture across jurisdictions — the core technical domain of the role
response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically capable senior engineer with genuine distributed-systems experience and early exposure to agentic tooling, but the profile falls substantially short of the CTO role's requirements on every executive dimension: there is no history of engineering org leadership, no production AI systems built and operated at financial consequence, and no depth in the credit, trading, or compliance infrastructure that defines this company's core technical domain. The gap is structural, not incremental — this is a role that demands someone who has already operated at executive scale in a regulated AI-native fintech context.
